EMERGENCY_NOW.label: Emergency now
EMERGENCY_NOW.urgency_message: Call emergency services now or have someone take you to emergency care immediately. Do not drive yourself if symptoms could worsen.
what_to_do_now: Stay with the patient if possible and monitor breathing and responsiveness.
Do not eat or drink if severe symptoms, possible stroke, severe injury, or reduced consciousness are present.
what_to_tell_provider: Main symptom and exact time symptoms started or were last known well.
Any chest pain, breathing difficulty, neurological symptoms, bleeding, seizure, allergic reaction, or loss of consciousness.
Relevant history, medications, allergies, pregnancy status, and what has already been tried.
red_flags_to_watch: Worsening chest pain or trouble breathing
Face drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty, sudden confusion, or vision loss
Collapse, seizure, severe bleeding, blue lips, or swelling of the tongue or throat
EMERGENCY_NOW.disclaimer: Educational prototype only. This is not a diagnosis, does not replace a clinician, and emergency symptoms require immediate professional help.

AE_TODAY.label: A&E / emergency department today
AE_TODAY.urgency_message: Seek same-day urgent emergency department or A&E assessment today.
what_to_do_now: Avoid driving yourself if symptoms are severe, worsening, or could impair alertness.
Escalate to emergency services immediately if red flags appear.
what_to_tell_provider: Symptom timeline, severity, and whether symptoms are worsening.
Associated symptoms such as vomiting, fever, breathing changes, bleeding, weakness, or fainting.
Medical history, medications, allergies, pregnancy possibility, and recent injuries or exposures.
red_flags_to_watch: New chest pain with shortness of breath, sweating, fainting, or pain spreading to arm, jaw, neck, or back
New weakness, speech trouble, confusion, seizure, stiff neck, rash, or severe headache
Severe breathing difficulty, blue lips, severe bleeding, or loss of consciousness
AE_TODAY.disclaimer: Educational prototype only. This is not a diagnosis, does not replace a clinician, and emergency symptoms require immediate professional help.

GP_URGENT.label: Urgent GP appointment
GP_URGENT.urgency_message: Contact a GP, primary care clinician, nurse line, or urgent clinic for prompt advice, ideally today or within 24 hours.
what_to_do_now: Book urgent primary care or call a nurse advice line.
Keep monitoring symptoms and avoid strenuous activity if symptoms are significant.
Escalate to emergency care if any red flag symptoms develop.
what_to_tell_provider: Main symptom, duration, severity, and recent change.
Any relevant negatives, such as no shortness of breath or no neurological symptoms if known.
Medical history, medicines, allergies, and pregnancy status if relevant.
red_flags_to_watch: Fainting, confusion, severe pain, breathing difficulty, blue lips, severe dehydration, or uncontrolled bleeding
Stroke-like symptoms or chest pain with radiation or shortness of breath
GP_URGENT.disclaimer: Educational prototype only. This is not a diagnosis, does not replace a clinician, and emergency symptoms require immediate professional help.

GP_ROUTINE.label: Routine GP appointment
GP_ROUTINE.urgency_message: Arrange a routine GP or primary care appointment, especially if symptoms persist, recur, or affect daily activities.
what_to_do_now: Book a routine appointment or use standard local care access.
Track symptoms, triggers, temperature, pain scores, and any new symptoms.
Escalate if symptoms worsen or red flags appear.
what_to_tell_provider: How long symptoms have been present and how they are changing.
Current medications, allergies, and relevant medical history.
red_flags_to_watch: New breathing difficulty, chest pain, weakness, confusion, fainting, severe fever, rash, or dehydration
Symptoms that become sudden, severe, or unusual for the patient
GP_ROUTINE.disclaimer: Educational prototype only. This is not a diagnosis, does not replace a clinician, and emergency symptoms require immediate professional help.

SELF_CARE.label: Self-care with monitoring
SELF_CARE.urgency_message: Self-care and monitoring may be reasonable based on the information provided, with clear escalation if symptoms change.
what_to_do_now: Rest, hydrate, and use usual self-care measures appropriate for the symptom.
Monitor symptom severity, duration, and new symptoms.
Seek professional care if symptoms persist, worsen, or red flags appear.
what_to_tell_provider: If care is later needed, share the symptom timeline, severity, triggers, and what changed.
Mention any medicines taken and any allergies.
Bring a list of relevant medical history and current medications.
red_flags_to_watch: Chest pain with shortness of breath, sweating, fainting, or pain spreading to arm, jaw, neck, or back
Stroke-like symptoms, sudden severe headache, seizure, confusion, stiff neck, rash, or head injury
Severe breathing difficulty, blue lips, severe bleeding, allergic swelling, or loss of consciousness
SELF_CARE.disclaimer: Educational prototype only. This is not a diagnosis, does not replace a clinician, and emergency symptoms require immediate professional help.
